prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='DWT_svr', seasonality_champion='DWT_svr', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: DWT_svr dominates both trend and seasonality capture with lowest slope error (3.3159) and highest seasonal correlation (0.7314). THETA leads overall RMSE (2266.50), while early/late horizon specialists differ (THETA vs FT_svr). With rmse_spread_ratio=1.41 and seasonality_corr_variance=0.864, models disagree significantly on both trend and seasonal patterns—dba_combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across the diverse model behaviors.
prompts.skeptic:

IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "DWT_svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "DWT_svr",
  "overall_champion": "THETA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "THETA", "late": "FT_svr"},
  "tier1_models": ["THETA", "ETS", "FT_svr", "rf", "ARIMA", "DWT_svr", "ONLY_FT_catboost", "ONLY_DWT_svr"],
  "tier2_models": ["ONLY_DWT_rf", "NaiveMovingAverage", "svr", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "NaiveSeasonal", "CWT_rf", "CWT_catboost"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.4104,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "DWT_svr dominates both trend and seasonality capture with lowest slope error (3.3159) and highest seasonal correlation (0.7314). THETA leads overall RMSE (2266.50), while early/late horizon specialists differ (THETA vs FT_svr). With rmse_spread_ratio=1.41 and seasonality_corr_variance=0.864, models disagree significantly on both trend and seasonal patterns—dba_combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across the diverse model behaviors."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0"],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=7 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. pattern_analyst_insights recommends dba_combination for high seasonality_corr_variance=0.864 and trend/seasonality champions (DWT_svr). With n_windows=3, shrinkage is critical—inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 provides regularization. THETA RMSE=2290 vs ONLY_CWT_svr RMSE=7297 shows rmse_spread_ratio=1.41, justifying rmse_focus preset. dba_combination handles phase-shifted predictions across model tiers."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set is diverse with 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), and 1 baseline (dba_combination) types. No leakage issues detected - baselines never leak, and weighted/selection strategies use past windows only per expanding rolling config (train_window=5). Universe leaderboard top candidates (trimmed_mean variants with scores ~0.83-0.84) have lower aggregate scores than current set members (scores 0.92-1.03). Candidate count of 5 is reasonable without redundancy. No changes needed.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: DWT_svr dominates both trend and seasonality capture with lowest slope error (3.3159) and highest seasonal correlation (0.7314). THETA leads overall RMSE (2266.50), while early/late horizon specialists differ (THETA vs FT_svr). With rmse_spread_ratio=1.41 and seasonality_corr_variance=0.864, models disagree significantly on both trend and seasonal patterns—dba_combination is recommended to leverage complementary strengths across the diverse model behaviors.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=7 indicates strong horizon heterogeneity requiring per-horizon selection methods. pattern_analyst_insights recommends dba_combination for high seasonality_corr_variance=0.864 and trend/seasonality champions (DWT_svr). With n_windows=3, shrinkage is critical—inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25 provides regularization. THETA RMSE=2290 vs ONLY_CWT_svr RMSE=7297 shows rmse_spread_ratio=1.41, justifying rmse_focus preset. dba_combination handles phase-shifted predictions across model tiers.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set is diverse with 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 2 weighted (exp_weighted_average_eta1.0_trim1.0, inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), and 1 baseline (dba_combination) types. No leakage issues detected - baselines never leak, and weighted/selection strategies use past windows only per expanding rolling config (train_window=5). Universe leaderboard top candidates (trimmed_mean variants with scores ~0.83-0.84) have lower aggregate scores than current set members (scores 0.92-1.03). Candidate count of 5 is reasonable without redundancy. No changes needed.
